relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Mark begins with the good message of Jesus Messiah, Son of the Divine.
A messenger prepares the way before him.
The wilderness voice calls for the Lord’s path to be made straight.
John’s baptism calls people into turning, realignment, and release.
Judea and Jerusalem come to the Jordan confessing misalignment.
John embodies wilderness-prophet simplicity.
He points beyond himself to the stronger one.
Water prepares; holy Spirit immerses.
Jesus comes from Nazareth and enters the Jordan waters.
The heavens tear open and Spirit descends like a dove.
The voice names beloved Son and divine delight.
Immediately Spirit drives him into wilderness.
Testing, wild animals, and messenger service surround him there.
After John is handed over, Jesus proclaims in Galilee.
Time is fulfilled; the Divine reign is near; turn, realign, and trust the good message.
Simon and Andrew are seen at their nets.
Jesus calls them to become fishers of people.
Immediately they leave nets and follow.
James and John are seen mending nets.
Immediately they leave boat, father, and hired workers to follow.
In Capernaum, Jesus teaches on Sabbath.
His teaching carries authority unlike ordinary scribal instruction.
A man in unclean spirit cries out in the synagogue.
The hidden force recognizes the Holy One.
Jesus commands silence and release.
The unclean spirit departs with a loud cry.
The people marvel at new teaching with authority.
The report spreads immediately through Galilee.
They leave the synagogue and enter Simon and Andrew’s house.
Simon’s mother-in-law lies with fever.
Jesus takes her hand, raises her, and service flows from healing.
At sunset, the sick and troubled are brought to him.
The city gathers at the door.
Healing and release spread, while recognition is kept silent.
Before dawn, Jesus withdraws to pray in a deserted place.
Simon and the others search for him.
They tell him everyone is seeking him.
He moves onward because proclamation is his purpose.
Galilee receives teaching, proclamation, and release.
A man asks whether Jesus is willing to cleanse.
Compassion reaches through touch: I am willing.
Immediately the man is cleansed.
Jesus sends him away with strict warning.
The cleansing is to be witnessed through priest and Moses’ instruction.
The man spreads the word, and Jesus remains outside while people come from everywhere.
